text.title: Launch Comms Kit Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Launch Comms Kit Template application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: All channel pieces MUST share ONE positioning sentence (≤140 chars); per-channel positioning variants are rejected.
rule.rationale: Drift across channels confuses early adopters and breaks measurement.

rule.statement: Kit MUST include a launch-day timeline (publish times per channel + retro slot); free-floating drafts without timeline are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without timeline the launch becomes a panic-publish; timeline lets the operator focus on response, not authoring.

rule.statement: Post-launch retro MUST be calendared at kit commit time (T+7); not 'we'll book it after launch'.
rule.rationale: Retros booked after launch never happen; pre-booking captures fresh signal.

rule.statement: Each piece MUST respect the host channel's shape (PH headline ≤60 chars, HN title rules, tweet thread ≤25 tweets, mailing-list subject ≤45 chars).
rule.rationale: Off-shape copy gets auto-throttled by platform algorithms.

rule.statement: Every product claim MUST cite verbatim source (changelog id, demo URL, customer quote with consent); marketing-prose claims are rejected.
rule.rationale: Anti-hallucination: LLM-generated launch copy invents features.
